overview: The Segmentation step segments the content of extracted text units into smaller parts, usually corresponding to sentences, using SRX 2.0 (Segmentation Rules eXchange) rules. It takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output. Text units flagged as non-translatable, text units with no content, and text units that are already segmented (unless overwrite is enabled) are not segmented.

segmentSource.description: When enabled, segments the source text of the text units using the specified SRX rules document. The SRX document path can use the variables ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir}.
notes: Text units flagged as non-translatable are not segmented.
Text units with no content are not segmented.

segmentTarget.description: When enabled, segments the existing target text of the text units using the specified SRX rules document, if there is text for the target locales being processed. Only the locales currently being processed are affected, even if the document contains more locales. The SRX document can be the same as for source segmentation. The path can use the variables ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir}.
notes: Only target locales being processed are affected, even if the document contains additional locales.

renumberCodes.description: When enabled, changes the IDs of inline codes in each resulting segment so they all start at '1'. This can be useful when working with translation memories where each segment restarts the IDs of its inline codes. The 'Restore original IDs to renumbered codes' option in the Desegmentation Step can be used to restore the original IDs when desegmenting.
notes: Warning: This option cannot work with documents where the ID values are not numeric, or not sequential.
Using this option when creating a translation kit requires a separate Desegmentation Step when merging back, and may not be possible with some formats that are segment-based like TTX.

checkSegments.description: When enabled, verifies that if there is a target available, all source segments have a corresponding target segment in the target content. This also verifies that both source and target have the same number of segments.
notes: This verification does not ensure that the content of a target segment is the translation of its corresponding source text. It only matches their segment ID.

trimSrcLeadingWS.description: Controls trimming of leading whitespace from source segments. A value of -1 means no trimming (default behavior).

trimSrcTrailingWS.description: Controls trimming of trailing whitespace from source segments. A value of -1 means no trimming (default behavior).

trimTrgLeadingWS.description: Controls trimming of leading whitespace from target segments. A value of -1 means no trimming (default behavior).

trimTrgTrailingWS.description: Controls trimming of trailing whitespace from target segments. A value of -1 means no trimming (default behavior).

forceSegmentedOutput.description: When enabled, forces file formats that can represent segmentation to show the segments, regardless of what is set in the filter configuration. For example, the XLIFF Filter by default outputs segments only for entries that had segments in the input XLIFF document. When this option is set, the filter's output option is changed to show segments as needed, even for text units that had no segments in the input. If this option is off, segments are shown only if the filter configuration allows it.

overwriteSegmentation.description: Controls behavior when input text is already segmented. When enabled, if a text unit is already segmented, it is re-segmented against the SRX rules specified in the step parameters. Previous segmentation will be lost. When disabled (and deepenSegmentation is also disabled), existing segmentation is preserved unchanged.
notes: This is one of three mutually exclusive segmentation behaviors: keep existing (both overwrite and deepen off), overwrite (this option on), or deepen (deepenSegmentation on).

deepenSegmentation.description: Controls behavior when input text is already segmented. When enabled, keeps existing segmentation but tries to segment existing segments further, creating more segments and text parts out of existing segments. Segmentation gets deeper only if the new SRX rules in the step parameters provide higher granularity than the original segmentation.
notes: This is one of three mutually exclusive segmentation behaviors: keep existing (both overwrite and deepen off), overwrite (overwriteSegmentation on), or deepen (this option on).

treatIsolatedCodesAsWhitespace.description: When enabled, treats isolated inline codes as whitespace during segmentation. This can affect where segment breaks are placed when inline codes appear between potential segments.

doNotSegmentIfHasTarget.description: When enabled, skips segmentation of a text unit if it already has target content. This can be used to preserve pre-existing aligned source-target pairs without applying segmentation.

limitations: Strict implementation of SRX syntax in Java is not possible. See the 'SRX and Java' page for details on the limitations.
Using the 'Renumber code IDs' option when creating a translation kit requires a separate Desegmentation Step when merging back, and may not be possible with some segment-based formats like TTX.
The renumber codes option cannot work with documents where inline code ID values are not numeric or not sequential.
processingNotes: Text units flagged as non-translatable are not segmented.
Text units with no content are not segmented.
Text units that are already segmented are not re-segmented unless the 'overwriteSegmentation' or 'deepenSegmentation' option is set.
SRX document paths support ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir} variable substitution.
The step uses SRX 2.0 rules, which define break and non-break positions using regular expression patterns.

The separation between text units is based on the structure of the original file format (e.g., each

element in HTML produces a separate text unit); this step further breaks down text unit content into segments.
